mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months signals rising inflation expectations, which erodes purchasing power and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive despite CPI increases exceeding 2% over 6 months, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over 3 months signals a tightening labor market, which leads to higher wages and increased consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor markets are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over 6 months signals rising production capacity, which leads to increased economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative despite INDPRO increases exceeding 2% over 6 months, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in the Real Estate Sales (RSAFS) over 3 months signals rising housing demand, which leads to increased economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in rising housing demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle, which leads to higher borrowing costs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive despite FEDFUNDS increases exceeding 1% over 3 months, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% decrease in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months signals falling inflation expectations, which leads to increased consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only when the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 5%.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL falls < 2% in 6 months and UNRATE < 5%
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in falling inflation periods with low unemployment are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Nonfarm Payrolls (PAYEMS) over 3 months signals a strengthening labor market, which leads to increased economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) is above 100.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 1% in 3 months and INDPRO > 100
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative despite PAYEMS increases exceeding 1% over 3 months and INDPRO above 100, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
